data.title: Input Layer and Encoding
data.description: Encode arithmetic expressions and linear equations as directed graphs. No node attributes — identity emerges from structure. Input layer immutable. Seed rules provided as bootstrap scaffold.
data.exit_condition: Expressions reliably converted to base graphs. Sample manually inspected and encoding confirmed sane.

encoding.numbers: Minimal binary trees. Open-length — depth determined by the number, no fixed bitwidth, no padding. Each number has exactly one structural representation. Internal edges are structural.
encoding.operators: Internal nodes with distinct arbitrary structural shapes — no node typing. Connect to operand subtree roots via operational edges. Four initial operators: +, -, *, /. New operators added as needed.
encoding.equality: Root node of equation expression tree. Distinct structural shape from arithmetic operators.
encoding.variable_x: Anonymous structural node with a distinct shape. Belongs to parameter structural family (separate from operand family). No special rule treatment — arithmetic rules simply do not match it.
encoding.structural_families: Three families: numbers (binary trees), operators (distinct shapes per operator), parameters (x and future variables). Families are structurally distinguishable to prevent accidental rule matches.
encoding.expression_tree: Operators as internal nodes, numbers and variables as leaves. Tree topology encodes evaluation order — deeper nodes resolve first. No explicit bracket nodes needed.
encoding.carry_propagation: Operational edge from operator to current bit position acts as cursor. Rule detaches and reattaches edge to next bit node. No extra marker node required.

label_format.arithmetic: Terminal value node only — e.g. 7. No wrapper.
label_format.equations: Solved form graph — e.g. x = 3. Irreducible — x = 3 does not collapse to 3.
label_format.collapse_rule: x = x collapses to x. 3 = 3 collapses to 3. x = 3 does not collapse — both sides are structurally distinct.

complexity_ladder: Level 2: One-step solving — e.g. x + 4 = 7, label: x = 3
Level 3: Two-step solving — e.g. 2x + 4 = 10, label: x = 3
Level 4: Variable on both sides — e.g. 2x + 4 = 3x - 1, label: x = 5
open_design_questions: Concrete structural shapes for operator nodes, parameter nodes, and carry marker — to be designed at PC implementation start
